changes_summary: Addressed all 6 flags: (1) Flat `## Step N:` format throughout — no phase headers. (2) Full-screen gating now checks `searchParams.has('timeline')` in addition to pathname, so timeline list view keeps normal layout. (3) Identified and suppressed ALL layout constraints: `container mx-auto` wrapper in LayoutMainContent.tsx:64, `SocialIcons` footer in Layout.tsx:128-134, pane margins, GlobalProcessingWarning, containerPadding/containerSpacing. (4) Corrected drag source — `MediaGalleryItem.tsx:324` already has `draggable={!isMobile}` with `handleDragStart` calling `setGenerationDragData()` at line 267. Extended the existing `GenerationDropData` protocol instead of inventing a new one. (5) Variant data extracted from the generation's primary variant at drag time via existing `image` prop data, adding `variantId` and `variantType` to `GenerationDropData`. No query-level changes needed. (6) Added `registerAsset` method to `DataProvider`/`SupabaseDataProvider` that persists to the `asset_registry` column (extracted from existing `uploadAsset` logic). Drop handler calls both `patchRegistry` (local) and `registerAsset` (DB persist).
questions: The `image` prop in MediaGalleryItem — does it reliably carry the variant_id for the displayed variant? If GenerationsPane shows generation-level items (not variants), we need to confirm where to get the primary variant ID at drag time. It might be in `image.metadata`, or we may need to look it up from the generation_variants table via the query cache.
The back bar — should it also show the project name, or just timeline name + back link? And should 'back' go to the timeline list (`/tools/video-editor`) or to the previous page (`navigate(-1)`)?
success_criteria: Video editor with `?timeline=` uses full viewport — no GlobalHeader, no SocialIcons footer, no `container mx-auto` constraint, no pane margins
Video editor WITHOUT `?timeline=` (timeline list) keeps normal layout with GlobalHeader and footer
Navigating away from /tools/video-editor restores normal layout completely
A minimal back bar (~32-40px) shows at top of editor with back link and timeline name
EditorPane dots are suppressed on the editor route
Generation items can be dragged from GenerationsPane and dropped onto the timeline to create clips, using the existing `application/x-generation` drag protocol
Generation items can be dragged onto the assets panel to register without creating a clip
Dropped assets include generationId and variantId in their AssetRegistryEntry
Dropped assets are persisted to the `asset_registry` DB column via registerAsset — survive page reload
Existing drag flows (asset-key from panel, file upload) continue working unchanged
Assets panel in PropertiesPanel is collapsed by default
Assets with generationId/variantId show a click-through action that opens MediaLightbox
No hardcoded color classes (zinc-*, gray-*, slate-*) remain in video editor components
Compact mode in EditorPane still works on non-editor routes
npm run build passes with no type errors
assumptions: The `image` prop in MediaGalleryItem contains enough data to extract the primary variant ID — likely via `image.metadata?.variant_id` or similar field. If not available directly, we may need to look up the variant from the generation data already in the query cache.
Variant assets reference the original generation storage URL directly (no re-upload) — if the generation is deleted, the asset shows a broken state, acceptable for v1.
The back bar is minimal: just back link + timeline name. Save/render status stays in existing preview/chrome areas.
The entire AssetPanel surface becomes a drop target with visual highlight indicator.
MediaLightbox can be rendered from within the video editor component tree without z-index or portal conflicts.
`GlobalProcessingWarning` can be safely hidden on the editor route without missing critical alerts — the editor has its own render/save status indicators.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
